m: KreukCheckride Proficiency Flight- Soft field takeoff: SAT - Power on stall: SAT - Power off stall: ST - - Soft field landing: more flare, don't let nose land. - Short field landing: SAT - Short field takeoff: SAT

KreukPrivate SE Initial Checkride- Congratulations on your checkride pass! It has been a pleasure flying with you and being your instructor. I wish you the best going forward. I hope to share a flight deck with you one day!
KreukInstrument Basics & Procedures- Flight back from checkride. - Congrats on your private checkride!

KreukPrivate Checkride Prep- Soft Field TO: SAT. Best one yet - Soft Field Landings: 4/5. The one that was unsat we just didnt hold the plane in ground effect so we just landed a little firm. - Congrats on your endorsement!

KreukPrivate Checkride Prep- TOLs at JZP - - Unusual attitudes: SAT - - Short Landing: - 1) 50 feet short. Took power out too soon but you didnt have a stall horn meaning you had energy to keep the plane off the runway. If you will be short, add power or go around - 2) SAT - 3) SAT - - Power Off Landing: - 1) turned base right away. We were right high on base/final and needed all flaps and a slip. Landed right around 1/3 down the runway. Make sure to turn base after losing 100 feet. Good job managing airspeed. - 2) flaps for and 80 kts on short final due to being high too long and having to push the nose over to get down. Still landed in 1/3 though. - 3) Landed flaps 10 and right where you wanted to - - Soft Landing: - 1) this was looking beautiful but unfortunately we had to do around due to a plane on the runway - 2) we were fairly high but also didnt keep the plane in ground effect, allowing it to land normally - 3) SAT - 4) never rounded out - - Short and PO landings are SAT. We just need to knock out a few more soft field landings

KreukPrivate Checkride Prep- All maneuvers SAT. We just need to clean up the performance landings and youll be set! - We reviewed all maneuvers in the prebrief - Short Field TO: SAT - Steep Turns: SAT 2/2 - Slow Flight: SAT. Just be careful going flaps 40 too fast. You could balloon. - POff Stall: SAT - POn Stall: SAT - Engine Fire: SAT - Engine out: SAT - Turns Around a Point: SAT - S-Turns: SAT - - Landings: - Soft Landings: - 1-3) more like normal landings and not soft field. Lap 2 needed aileron and rudder inputs. We landed left of CL and with the nose pointed right of CL due to the right cross wind. Lap 3 was much better corrections you just didnt hold the plane in ground effect. It just landed instead of bleeding off airspeed for a smooth touchdown -

KreukPrivate Checkride Prep- TOLs at PDK - - Soft TO: When staying in ground effect watch out to not push the nose over too much to descend and touch back down on the runway. - - Soft Landings: - 1) this was perfect until ground effect. You had the plane in a nose up attitude in ground effect. However, you then kept adding aft pressure causing a balloon - 2) I called a go around. Too slow. Hit 60 kts on short final. - 3) hold the plane in ground effect longer. We could have landed smoother - - Short Landings: Remember to move your aiming point further down the runway if we are using the 1000 footers. We dont want to keep the plane in ground effect for 700-800 feet. - 1) SAT - 2) 100 feet short. Either add power or go around. Never land short. - 3) SAT. Landed flat on all 3 wheels at once. Still try to land on the mains first - - PO Landing: - 1) Way too short. It was hot and humid out. Aircraft performance was not good. Also review the Archer Supplement. First step is controlling airspeed at 80 kts. We were 90 kts. Also we had a wide base. When performance is bad we need to get to the runway quicker. - 2) This approach had a similar ground path. We were a little tighter on the base but not by much. However, you did excellent controlling airspeed. We had to use flaps 40 and slip to make the first 1/3. It goes to show how important airspeed management is!

KreukNight- After a week of trying to get this flight in every day the weather finally held up! - - Great job with navigating and with the night landings. All were SAT - - Your mock checkride will be tomorrow afternoon.

KreukMock Private Checkride- Pilotage/Dead Reckoning: SAT. Found your points. Make your point 10-15nm, this give you enough space between each point but not too far away where you can veer off course. - - Steep turns: SAT. Great job getting the CRAAC checklist completed and did the maneuver within standards. - - Slow flight: UNSAT. Region of reverse command, you got this mixed up and though power for airspeed and pitch for altitude. This is for cruise flight. Region of reverse command is pitch for airspeed and power for altitude. - - Power off stall: SAT. Just be a little faster taking out flaps to 10 for the stall recovery. Keep practicing the recovery. - - Power on stall: SAT. Great job on the stall but watch getting off heading when recovering. - - Normal landing: SAT - - Soft field takeoff: Once the plane is in the air, pitch the nose down to hold ground effect and speed up to 64 KIAS, then climb out and slowly retract flaps. - - Soft field landing: Great job with this. You held the nose wheel to protect it from the landing. - - Short field takeoff: You want to use all available runway, stop, go full power while on the breaks. Rotate at 55 KIAS, speed up to 60 and the climb up at 64 KIAS. Once over the 50 ft obstacle, then retract flaps. - - Short field landing: Find an aiming point and then touchdown point. The aiming point is what you should fly to and be able to land on the landing point that you choose. - - Turns around a point: UNSAT. - Did turns around a point on the board since the flight was cut short due to storm in the vicinity. - The altitude you want to be at is 600-100ft AGL. To find field elevation, click the knob on the MFD and on the top left corner shows what the field elevation is. For the airspeed, you want to be 90 KIAS, the tolerances are +- 100ft and +-10 KIAS. - - You are on the right track, just a couple things to clean up and then you should be good to go for your checkride!
KreukSolo XCs-Solo XC number 2 - You have 10 hours of solo so we will do the night XC flight next!
KreukSolo XCs- First solo XC! We will do it again tomorrow morning!

KreukSolo XC Eval- KPDK-KAND - When you are doing the checklist, occasionally look back at your instruments to make sure you haven’t veered off course. - Just the nearest page to help you figure out how far you are from the airport when you are getting flight following. - When you veered off your heading and you hit the next point and it’s farther away, make the correct to get closer so for the next point, you’ll be back on course. - Nice job staying ahead of the plane by getting the approach checklist completed. - Made the correct entry for the correct runway (based off the winds from the ASOS). - Maintained situational awareness and made CTAF calls. - - KCPP - Nice job tuning and ID the VOR on the ground. - Identified correctly what radial you were on. - Emergency: Engine roughness/failure. When you diverted to KEBA, make sure you hop on their CTAF to make calls to traffic in the area. - Triangulating VORs: Make sure you double check which VOR frequency you were on. - For the CTAF calls, you want to tell traffic how you are going to enter the pattern for each call you make. - - KPDK - Zoom out on MFD so this helps you maintain situational awareness. - - Great job today! Enjoy the solo xc!

KreukDual XC 2- All lights on crossing runways - Navlog: SAT. Youve got a solid grasp on this. The only hiccup was at the beginning. You said you had I-85 to your left but it was to your right. Look left, forward and right to verify you have the correct highway/point. - Great job with transition request w LZU tower and flight following calls! - Once AND is in sight, knock out the approach checklist. - Actually pull out the paper checklist for normal checklist items. Thats how you forget things! - Mixture was leaned on final. Forget to richen on approach checklist and never went full rich configuring. Cannot miss it twice. - After landing on RWY 23 you said "off 3L taxi back via Alpha". Think before calling. There isnt a 3L runway at AND. Also, if you dont know your taxi route, figure it out before saying it over CTAF. Taxi back to RWY 23 is "taxi RWY 23 via Alpha, cross RWY 17 at A". Dont say 'off runway'. Use 'clear of runway'. Same with taking off. "Taking off runway 23". Phrases like "for 23" can be confused with "4 2 3" which doesnt make sense. - You must look left and right and clear a runway before crossing, especially at a untowered airport. Never cross the hold short line without doing this. This is how accidents happen. Radios are not required, or people may be on the wrong frequency. - VOR usage: SAT - Engine roughness: SAT. Great job getting to KEBA and landings in the first 1/3 - Engine fire: UNSAT. Started using the "electrical fire" checklist not the "engine fire" checklist. Know your emergency checklists by memory. Pitch for 120, not 76. Once fire is out then you want to pitch for 76. - Alternator failed: we continued to CPP for a full stop. Great job helping me with the checklist. You did the flying. You saw how I notified CTAF of the alternator failure in case we lost comms. - - Down arrow is for: - Did not do cruise checklist again today after PDK or AND without reminder - Mixture leaned on final at AND before I added it to full rich. Did not pull out approach checklist. Did it from memory. - Radio calls at AND and not clearing runway before crossing during taxi - Emergency checklist - I need these memorized before I can send you on a solo XC. We will review these prior to the solo Friday. I want to hear the engine fire during start, engine fire airborne, electrical fire, engine failure and engine roughness checklists all without you looking at the checklist. Every second counts during an emergency, especially the fires. - Great job with navlog, planning airport entries, flight following and with the alternator failure today! I have full confidence you will pass the eval tomorrow without issue

KreukDual XC 1- Excellent NavLog - Only thing was you restarted the timer a few miles before the first point did not document the GS and time en route. Everything else was good. - Flight following was SAT too - - VOR SAT - - Engine Failure - to KEBA. For the ABCDE, if youre already near the airport go to CTAF, not Guard. For slip ailerons into wind opposite rudder. The wind indication on the bottom left of the G500 shows this. Landed right on 1000 footers! Good job. - Rough engine: work to memorize emergency checklists. Regular checklist like the cruise, approach, etc should always be done with the checklist line by line. But, emergency items need to be memorized because sometimes you cannot waste time, example a fire or serious rough engine. Always go back to reference to make sure you completed all items though after doing the memory items. - - The big kicker for this flight was me reminding you to complete the cruise checklist after leaving PDK, AND and CPP. You cannot forget that. - Forgot items in the approach checklist. Actually pull out and use the checklist for cruise and approach, not just from memory. - Foggles: SAT - Diversions: SAT

KreukDual XC 1- Dual XC (attempt 1) - - NavLog: Time is on the G500. We will start it before takeoff for the first leg. - Watch altitude and heading. The first 1/3 of the PDK to AND leg we kept getting off. However, once the workload was reduced after leveling off and establishing flight following, you did well with this. - Flight following: just keep practicing to make the calls smoother. Its good to practice with other students if you need to. Once you get the flow down it becomes a lot easier. - Dont forget to write down the GS and minutes passing every leg - One leg was 24 miles. You were able to find it but aim for a 15 mile max between points so you dont get lost - Once we were close to AND we were about 6 miles south from the direct path from PDK to AND. Part of this was slightly drifting right consistently at the beginning, but also because the wind and velocity that was forecasted was slightly off from what we experienced, and what we dealt with pushed us south of course. You were still able to easily find all your point and AND so its good to see how the difference in the forecast and actual winds are! - - AND entry SAT - VOR usage: SAT. You did great flying inbound to the ELW VOR on the 360 radial. We also triangulated our position with the AHN VOR. Practice tuning and IDing VORs so its a little more fluid. - Engine failure scenario: I recommend spending time memorizing the emergency checklist and flows. ABCDE. Dont forget to do the shutdown checklist where all fuel items are shut down before touch down. Also, if youre right near an airport, go to their CTAF instead of Guard since you need to communicate with pattern traffic. You were able to land safely at KEBA which was great! - - We then noticed the oil pressure was fully high in the red. We diverted to AHN. We monitored oil temp as that would be an indication that we may need to shut the engine down to prevent a fire.
